cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828 mainly. The options are A) he was in poor health, B) he almost ran out of money, C) his co-workers argued with him, D) he spent all his money on his bills. First, I need to refer back to the provided text to find the relevant information. Let me read through the passage again and focus on the part around 1828. The passage says: "Babbage continued his work in London for four years. Then his health broke down, and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done." So, when he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources (which sounds like running out of money), had unpaid bills, and co-workers left. The question is asking for the main reason he couldn't continue his research. Looking at the options: A is about poor health. But the health breakdown happened before 1828, leading to a long holiday abroad. He came back in 1828, so the health issue was prior. So maybe A is not the main reason in 1828. The text says that when he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources, i.e., no money. Then his co-workers left him,
